title: Macron says 'everything' must be done to prevent new war in Lebanon
subhed: The French president said that Lebanese authorities had given him 'their commitment' to take control of Hezbollah positions, as France dispatches humanitarian aid.

title: Kurdish factions could weaken Iran's regime from within
subhed: As US-Israeli strikes continue, attention is turning to Iran's Kurdish units, now united in an unprecedented coalition. But the Kurds' history, marked by fragile alliances and betrayals, fuels both hope and caution.
byline: By Ghazal Golshiri and Allan Kaval

title: Israel bombs Beirut after telling 700,000 residents to leave
subhed: The evacuation order gave residents of the Lebanese capital's southern suburbs just hours to leave their homes.

title: How Israel and the US are waging their most intense air war in decades on Iran
subhed: The coordinated US-Israeli forces have devised 'unprecedented' operations to establish their military dominance over Iran, destroy its missile launchers and factories and gain control of their adversary's airspace.

title: Nepal's youth, long forced abroad for work, look to elections for fresh start
subhed: Six months after the Gen Z uprising that brought down the government, many young Nepalis hope the March 5 legislative elections will give the country, plagued by unemployment, new impetus. More than 2,000 young people leave the country every day.

title: France, Italy and Greece to coordinate 'dispatch of military assets' to Mediterranean

How Iran secretly acquired facial recognition technology through a Russian company
subhed: An investigation coordinated by Forbidden Stories, in partnership with Le Monde, reveals that an Iranian company purchased and then resold the Russian surveillance software FindFace to various entities within the regime.

title: In the West Bank's Qaryout, a Palestinian village is besieged by settlers
subhed: Two brothers were killed during an attack by Israeli settlers. As war with Iran rages, settlement expansion in the West Bank is continuing at an unprecedented pace, with almost total impunity.

title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhed: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.
byline: By Chloé Hoorman and Marie Jégo
